title: Warnings and diagnostics deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific deterministic MCQ and fill-gap evidence for Warnings and diagnostics; human review required.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Warnings and diagnostics: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A program with no compiler warnings is guaranteed to be defined, correct, and portable.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat clean diagnostics as bounded tool evidence, not proof; compile with strict warnings and still test behavior, contracts, portability, and undefined-behavior risks.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Treat clean diagnostics as bounded tool evidence, not proof; compile with strict warnings and still test behavior, contracts, portability, and undefined-behavior risks.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Treat clean diagnostics as bounded tool evidence, not proof; compile with strict warnings and still test behavior, contracts, portability, and undefined-behavior risks.
A program with no compiler warnings is guaranteed to be defined, correct, and portable.

answer.explanation: Treat clean diagnostics as bounded tool evidence, not proof; compile with strict warnings and still test behavior, contracts, portability, and undefined-behavior risks.

items.title: Warnings and diagnostics: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing C checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: A program with no compiler warnings is guaranteed to be defined, correct, and portable.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'strict warnings'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Treat clean diagnostics as bounded tool evidence, not proof; compile with strict warnings and still test behavior, contracts, portability, and undefined-behavior risks.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact C token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.
